text.title: Prototype Iteration Log Template Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Prototype Iteration Log Template. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every row carries exactly six sections: problem, hypothesis, variant, evidence, outcome, next; rows missing any section are rejected.
rule.rationale: Free-shape iteration logs decay within weeks; fixed shape makes rows comparable across sprints and trainable for agents.

rule.statement: Hypothesis text is in the form 'IF <change> THEN <observable outcome>'; vague intent ('improve UX') is rejected.
rule.rationale: Falsifiable hypotheses allow real outcomes; vague intent guarantees 'it kind of worked'.

rule.statement: Every row carries an evidence_link (URL, repo, doc) backing the outcome; opinion-only outcomes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Iteration logs without evidence become folklore; the link lets future readers audit the call.

rule.statement: Outcome is one of (kept | rolled-back | shipped-with-changes); ambiguous 'partial' outcomes are split into two rows.
rule.rationale: Ambiguous outcomes hide regret; the trinary forces a clear next step.

rule.statement: Owner field is a named handle; row carries iteration_date. Rows older than 60 days without a follow-up surface a warning.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no follow-up; dated rows let the log self-audit.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do NOT run this methodology — emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition and route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above for flat methodologies), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-prototype-iteration-log-template.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
